contentBlocks.original:
वैशाखे मासि कृष्णायां दशम्यां मन्दवासरे।
पूर्वाभाद्रप्रभूताय मङ्गलं श्रीहनूमते ॥ १ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
vaiśākhe māsi kṛṣṇāyāṃ daśamyāṃ mandavāsare.
pūrvābhādraprabhūtāya maṅgalaṃ śrīhanūmate .. 1 ..
contentBlocks.translation:
Mangalam to Sri Hanuman,
Who was born in the month of Visaka,
On the tenth day of the rising moon,
On a Saturday on Poorva bhadra star.
contentBlocks.meanings: वैशाखे (vaiśākhe) – in the month of Vaiśākha; मासि (māsi) – in the month; कृष्णायां (kṛṣṇāyām) – in the dark fortnight; दशम्यां (daśamyām) – on the tenth day; मन्दवासरे (mandavāsare) – on a Saturday (manda:slow, dull (Saturday) | vāsare:on the day); पूर्वाभाद्रप्रभूताय (pūrvābhādraprabhūtāya) – born under Pūrvabhādra star (pūrvābhādra:Pūrvabhādra (star) | prabhūtāya:born to); मङ्गलं (maṅgalaṃ) – auspiciousness; श्रीहनूमते (śrīhanūmate) – to Sri Hanuman (śrī:revered, auspicious | hanūmate:to Hanuman);

contentBlocks.original:
करुणारसपूर्णाय फलापूपप्रियाय च ।
माणिक्यहारकण्ठाय मङ्गलं श्रीहनूमते ॥ २ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
karuṇārasapūrṇāya phalāpūpapriyāya ca .
māṇikyahārakaṇṭhāya maṅgalaṃ śrīhanūmate .. 2 ..
contentBlocks.translation:
Mangalam to Sri Hanuman,
Who has respect for his teachers,
Who likes sweets prepared out of fruits,
And whose different hands are decorated by gems.
contentBlocks.meanings: करुणारसपूर्णाय (karuṇārasapūrṇāya) – full of compassion (karuṇā:compassion | rasa:essence | pūrṇāya:full of); फलापूपप्रियाय (phalāpūpapriyāya) – fond of fruit sweets (phala:fruit | āpūpa:sweets | priyāya:fond of); च (ca) – and; माणिक्यहारकण्ठाय (māṇikyahārakaṇṭhāya) – neck adorned with gem necklace (māṇikya:gem | hāra:necklace | kaṇṭhāya:to the neck); मङ्गलं (maṅgalaṃ) – auspiciousness; श्रीहनूमते (śrīhanūmate) – to Sri Hanuman (śrī:revered | hanūmate:to Hanuman);

contentBlocks.original:
सुवर्चलाकलत्राय चतुर्भुजधराय च ।
उष्ट्रारूढाय वीराय मङ्गलं श्रीहनूमते ॥ ३ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
suvarcalākalatrāya caturbhujadharāya ca .
uṣṭrārūḍhāya vīrāya maṅgalaṃ śrīhanūmate .. 3 ..
contentBlocks.translation:
Mangalam to Sri Hanuman,
Who is the consort of Suvarchala,
Who has four arms,
And who is the hero who rides,
On a beam of light.
contentBlocks.meanings: सुवर्चलाकलत्राय (suvarcalākalatrāya) – to the consort of Suvarchala (suvarcalā:Suvarchala | kalatrāya:to the consort); चतुर्भुजधराय (caturbhujadharāya) – to the four-armed bearer (catur:four | bhuja:arms | dharāya:to the bearer); च (ca) – and; उष्ट्रारूढाय (uṣṭrārūḍhāya) – to the camel-rider (uṣṭra:camel | ārūḍhāya:to the rider); वीराय (vīrāya) – to the hero; मङ्गलं (maṅgalaṃ) – auspiciousness; श्रीहनूमते (śrīhanūmate) – to Sri Hanuman (śrī:Sri | hanūmate:to Hanuman);

contentBlocks.original:
दिव्यमङ्गलदेहाय पीताम्बरधराय च ।
तप्तकाञ्चनवर्णाय मङ्गलं श्रीहनूमते ॥ ४ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
divyamaṅgaladehāya pītāmbaradharāya ca .
taptakāñcanavarṇāya maṅgalaṃ śrīhanūmate .. 4 ..
contentBlocks.translation:
Mangalam to Sri Hanuman,
Who has a blessed holy body,
Who wears the yellow silk,
And who is of the colour of molten gold.
contentBlocks.meanings: दिव्यमङ्गलदेहाय (divyamaṅgaladehāya) – to the divine auspicious body (divya:divine | maṅgala:auspicious | dehāya:to the body); पीताम्बरधराय (pītāmbaradharāya) – to the wearer of yellow garments (pīta:yellow | ambara:garment | dharāya:to the wearer); च (ca) – and; तप्तकाञ्चनवर्णाय (taptakāñcanavarṇāya) – to the one of molten gold color (tapta:molten | kāñcana:gold | varṇāya:to the one of color); मङ्गलं (maṅgalaṃ) – auspiciousness; श्रीहनूमते (śrīhanūmate) – to Sri Hanuman (śrī:Sri | hanūmate:to Hanuman);

contentBlocks.translation:
Mangalam to Sri Hanuman,
Who is known to save his devotees,
Who destroyed the sorrow of Janaki,
And who has the purest eyes of the world.
contentBlocks.meanings: भक्तरक्षणशीलाय (bhaktarakṣaṇaśīlāya) – to the protector of devotees (bhakta:devotee | rakṣaṇa:protection | śīlāya:to the one with nature); जानकीशोकहारिणे (jānakīśokahāriṇe) – remover of Janaki's sorrow (jānakī:Janaki | śoka:sorrow | hāriṇe:to the remover); ज्वलत्पावकनेत्राय (jvalatpāvakanetrāya) – to the one with fiery eyes (jvalat:fiery | pāvaka:fire | netrāya:to the one with eyes); मङ्गलं (maṅgalaṃ) – auspiciousness; श्रीहनूमते (śrīhanūmate) – to Lord Hanuman (śrī:revered | hanūmate:to Hanuman);

contentBlocks.translation:
Mangalam to Sri Hanuman,
Who lives in the shores of Pampa river,
Who saved the life of Lakshmana,
And who is in the throat of the whole world.
contentBlocks.meanings: पम्पातीरविहाराय (pampātīravihārāya) – to the one dwelling on Pampa's shore (pampā:Pampa | tīra:shore | vihāra:dwelling | āya:to the one); सौमित्रिप्राणदायिने (saumitriprāṇadāyine) – to the giver of life to Lakshmana (saumitri:Lakshmana | prāṇa:life | dāyine:to the giver); सृष्टिकारणभूताय (sṛṣṭikāraṇabhūtāya) – to the cause of creation (sṛṣṭi:creation | kāraṇa:cause | bhūtāya:to the one who is); मङ्गलं (maṅgalaṃ) – auspiciousness; श्रीहनूमते (śrīhanūmate) – to Lord Hanuman (śrī:Lord | hanūmate:to Hanuman);

contentBlocks.translation:
Mangalam to Sri Hanuman,
Who lived in the forests of Rampa,
Who lived with pleasant happy people,
And who is in the throat of the whole world.
contentBlocks.meanings: रम्भा (rambhā) – Rambha; वन (vana) – forest; विहाराय (vihārāya) – for the enjoyment; गन्धमादन (gandhamādana) – Gandhamadana; वासिने (vāsine) – to the resident; सर्व (sarva) – all; लोक (loka) – world; एक (eka) – one; नाथाय (nāthāya) – to the lord; मङ्गलं (maṅgalaṃ) – auspiciousness; श्रीहनूमते (śrīhanūmate) – to Lord Hanuman (śrī:revered | hanūmate:to Hanuman);

contentBlocks.original:
पञ्चाननाय भीमाय कालनेमिहराय च ।
कौण्डिन्यगोत्रजाताय मङ्गलं श्रीहनूमते ॥ ८ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
pañcānanāya bhīmāya kālanemiharāya ca .
kauṇḍinyagotrajātāya maṅgalaṃ śrīhanūmate .. 8 ..
contentBlocks.translation:
Mangalam to Sri Hanuman,
Who has five faces, who is gross,
Who killed Kalanemi,
And who belonged to the Koundinya clan.
contentBlocks.meanings: पञ्चाननाय (pañcānanāya) – to the five-faced (pañca:five | ānana:face | āya:to); भीमाय (bhīmāya) – to the formidable; कालनेमिहराय (kālanemiharāya) – to the slayer of Kalanemi (kālanemi:Kalanemi | harāya:to the slayer); च (ca) – and; कौण्डिन्यगोत्रजाताय (kauṇḍinyagotrajātāya) – to the one born in Koundinya clan (kauṇḍinya:Koundinya | gotra:clan | jāta:born | āya:to); मङ्गलं (maṅgalaṃ) – auspiciousness; श्रीहनूमते (śrīhanūmate) – to Sri Hanuman (śrī:Sri | hanūmate:to Hanuman);

contentBlocks.translation: Thus ends the Śrī Hanumān Maṅgalāṣṭakam.
contentBlocks.meanings: इति (iti) – thus; श्री (śrī) – auspicious; हनुमान् (hanumān) – Hanuman; मङ्गलाष्टकम् (maṅgalāṣṭakam) – auspicious eight-verse hymn (मङ्गल:auspicious | अष्टकम्:eight-verse hymn);
